title: Clock-free graphs are clean
statement_text: The class of clock-free graphs is clean.
context_text: A clock is a graph consisting of an induced cycle $C$ and a vertex not in $C$ with at least two non-adjacent neighbours in $C$. A class $\mathcal{C}$ of graphs is clean if there exists $f:\mathbb{N}\to\mathbb{N}$ such that for all $t$, every $t$-clean graph $G\in\mathcal{C}$ satisfies $\mathrm{tw}(G)\leq f(t)$. The conjecture was posed in prior joint work [1] by Abrishami, Alecu, Chudnovsky, Hajebi, Spirkl, and Vušković, where a weaker version (for (clock, prism, pyramid)-free graphs) was proved.
attributed_to: Abrishami, Alecu, Chudnovsky, Hajebi, Spirkl, and Vušković
paper_contribution: Proves the conjecture in full (Theorem 1.1): every clock-free graph of large treewidth contains, as an induced subgraph, one of the basic obstructions — a complete graph, a subdivision of a wall, or the line graph of a subdivision of a wall.
paper_title: Induced subgraphs and tree decompositions XIV. Non-adjacent neighbours in a hole

title: Conjecture 1.3
statement_text: For every fixed $t \geq 1$, the family of $t$-clock-free graphs is clean.
context_text: A $t$-clock is a clock consisting of a hole $C$ and a vertex $v$ such that there are two neighbours $x, y \in V(C)$ of $v$ where the distance between $x$ and $y$ along $C$ is at least $t$. The main result of the paper (Theorem 1.1) establishes cleanness for $t = 2$ (all clocks); Conjecture 1.3 proposes that cleanness persists even when clocks with close non-adjacent pairs are allowed, for every fixed threshold $t$.
paper_title: Induced subgraphs and tree decompositions XIV. Non-adjacent neighbours in a hole
